content.text: {"overview":"This B minor fugue is a rigorous, large-scale contrapuntal work spanning 76 bars, traversing a carefully planned cycle of diatonic pivot-chord modulations through the closely related keys of F# minor, D major, G# minor, E minor, and A major before returning emphatically to the tonic. Its most distinctive feature is the progressive intensification of contrapuntal density—from the three-voice exposition through chromatic sequential episodes to a climactic stretto and an extraordinary five-voice final cadence that crowns the entire architecture.","cross_piece_tags":["fugue","counterpoint","circle_of_fifths","chromatic_mediant","tragic","dramatic","modal_mixture","pedal_point"]}
